steps.title: Identify the critical signal
steps.content: The passage shows that something caused ecologists to “express reservations” about the traditional descriptions' utility. Reservations—skepticism about a theory—arise when new findings contradict the old theory.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The ecologists' reservations arise from a mismatch between what traditional descriptions predict and what recent observations show. The blank must name that type of mismatch. Substitute each choice:
  • Discrepancies between (A) — "Discrepancies between traditional descriptions… and recent empirical observations" = contradictions or mismatches between two things. This is exactly what would prompt reservations about a theory's usefulness. ✓
  • Proclamations of (B) — "Proclamations of traditional descriptions" = announcements or declarations; describes confident assertions, not a conflict between old and new data.
  • Recurrences of (C) — "Recurrences of traditional descriptions" = the descriptions happening again repeatedly; this would reinforce the traditional descriptions, not cause doubt.
  • Affinities between (D) — "Affinities between traditional descriptions and recent observations" = similarities or connections; agreement between old and new data would support, not undermine, the descriptions' utility.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
